details.text: Derivative: انْفَطَرَتْ from اِنْفَطَرَ – اِنْفِطَار and means to tear something wide open or apart across its length.
Derivative: إِذَا is a means of drawing attention to say “when” to a future event. It can also mean “all of a sudden”.
Derivative: حَافِظِينَ refers to the Angels that record the deeds. It comes from حَفِظَ which means to protect or guard something so that it is preserved.
Derivative: كِرَامًا is the plural of كَرِيم and means honourable and noble i.e., they have integrity and can be securely trusted.

Derivative: كَاتِبِينَ means “they are writing down” and the use of the اِسْمُ الفاعِل (Ism ul-Faa’il – Noun of the doer) instead of the verb يَكْتُبُونَ (they are writing) implies that they are constantly and continuously writing and recording.

Derivative: الْأَبْرَارَ is the plural of بَرٌّ (Barr) which means “righteous”. Angels were referred to as بَرَرَةٍ (Bararah) in Surah ‘Abasa which is also the plural of بَرٌّ (Barr). بَرَرَةٍ (Bararah) isجَمْعُ الكَثْرَة (Jam’u l-Kathrah) which means it’s the plural that is used when you are mentioning the plural that implies a large number whilst أَبْرَار (Abraar) is جَمْعُ القِلَّة (Jam’u l-Qillah) which a plural that implies a fewer number. That is because the Angels are righteous on mass (بَرَرَةٍ), whilst there are less righteous (أَبْرَار) among humans.
Derivative: نَعِيمٍ (Na’eem) is in the form of فَعِيل (Fa’eel) and implies a continuous favour, blessing and bliss i.e., comfort, easy, luxury and all that which is pleasing.
Derivative: إِنَّ is used in the Arabic language to remove doubt from the statement that follows it and means: “There is no doubt about it that …”.

Derivative: الْفُجَّارَ refers to those who openly and shamelessly commit sins without any concern. The use of the noun الْفُجَّارَ (Fujjaar) instead of a verb indicates that this describes a state or condition that has become stable and settled.
Derivative: يَصْلَوْنَ from صَلَى – يَصْلِي which means to be thrown or cast in or exposed to the fire.
- يَوْمَ الدِّينِ is the day in which payment and recompense that is due will be made and exacted i.e., the day of judgment.
Derivative: مَا (Maa) is a stronger form of negation than لَيْسَ (Laysa). When it is connected to بِ it an even stronger form of negation.

Derivative: غَائِبِينَ means that they will be “absent”. With مَا (i.e., negation) proceeding it and بِ attached to it مَا هُمْ عَنْهَا بِغَائِبِينَ, it literally means: “They will never be absent from it” i.e., they will never be able to escape from the Jaheem (hellfire).
Derivative: أَدْرَا to come to know or find out about something through clues and indications.

ثُمَّ مَا أَدْرَاكَ مَا يَوْمُ الدِّينِ

- The repetition of the verse and question here intensifies the significance and the horrific or tremendous magnitude associated with the subject of the question. Repetition in general is used to provide greater emphasis to the subject matter.
Derivative: تَمْلِكُ from مَلَكَ – يَمْلِكُ which means to have possession, control or authority i.e., to have the power or capability to do something.
Derivative: انْتَثَرَتْ from اِنْتَثَرَ which means to fall and then scatter and disperse. It is the image of something like marbles falling and scattering all over the place.
Derivative: الْكَوَاكِبُ is the plural of كَوْكَب which has been used to refer to the large stars and planets which are fixed and used for navigation.
Derivative: فُجِّرَتْ from فَجَّرَ – تَفْجِيِر which means to explode, burst out or erupt.

Derivative: بُعْثِرَتْ is the passive voice form of بَعْثَرَ means to turn something upside down to extract something, where the bottom and hidden becomes the top and apparent. Here it means for the graves to be turned upside down and their contents brought out.

Derivative: قَدَّمَتْ means to send forward. It can refer to the good or bad deeds that someone has done i.e., it is recorded to be held account for on the Day of Judgement. It can also refer to the first things you did in life.

Derivative: أَخَّرَتْ means to be left behind. It can refer to those acts which were not undertaken and left behind when the opportunity was there for them. It also refers to the opportunities to sin which were left behind and not undertaken. It can also refer to the last things you did in life. A third understanding is that it can refer to what you left behind in terms of good and bad which has an impact and left consequences after you have passed.
Derivative: غَرَّ means to delude or deceive and is used when somebody who is unaware or careless and is taken advantage of and conned or beguiled.

Derivative: إِنْسَانُ (human being) has been argued to have come from different roots. One is نَسِيَ which means to forget which is a fundamental characteristic of the human being. Another root is أَنِسَ – أُنْس which refers to the sociable and affectionate quality that is fundamental to a human being.
Derivative: سَوَّى means to balance and even something out, to fashion and fine tune something until it is sound and stable.
Derivative: عَدَلَ means to provide balance and proportion.
Derivative: رَكَّبَ – تَرْكِيب which means to put something on top of something else, to mount something or to assemble and put something together.
Derivative: صُورَةٍ means “a form” and refers to all objects that can be distinguished from each other by the eye.

Derivative: الدِّينِ is from دَانَ – يَدِينُ which means to take a loan and دَيْن is a loan. It relates to giving someone or taking from someone a specific amount of something that is due back. As such الدِّينِ (Ad-Deen) refers here to the recompense that will be exacted.
Derivative: تُكَذِّبُونَ from كَذَّبَ – تَكْذِيب and it means to call or declare something a lie, or to deny its veracity and truth.
